message:
fix: update kcp mission to operator-based install (#1977)

Replace the plain Helm-based kcp installation with the recommended
operator-based method per kcp maintainer guidance. The new mission
installs cert-manager, etcd-druid, and kcp-operator as prerequisites,
then deploys RootShard, FrontProxy, and Kubeconfig CRs. Includes
updated uninstall, upgrade, and troubleshooting sections.

Ref: https://docs.kcp.io/kcp/v0.30/setup/production/

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -5,82 +5,217 @@
   "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
   "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
   "mission": {
-    "title": "Install and Configure Kcp on Kubernetes",
-    "description": "kcp is a Kubernetes-like control plane designed for managing multiple isolated clusters, known as workspaces. It enables API service providers to centrally offer APIs and facilitates easy API consumption for users within their workspaces. Installing kcp allows you to leverage its capabilities for multi-tenant applications and services.",
+    "title": "Install and Configure Kcp on Kubernetes (Operator-Based)",
+    "description": "kcp is a Kubernetes-like control plane designed for managing multiple isolated clusters, known as workspaces. The kcp-operator is the recommended way to deploy kcp in production: it offers more features than plain Helm charts and can actively reconcile missing or changed resources on its own. This mission installs kcp using the operator-based method, including cert-manager for TLS, etcd-druid for etcd management, and the kcp-operator for deploying RootShard, FrontProxy, and admin access.",
     "type": "deploy",
     "status": "completed",
     "steps": [
       {
-        "title": "Add the kcp Helm repository",
-        "description": "Add the official kcp Helm repository to your Helm configuration:
```bash
helm repo add kcp https://kcp-dev.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
```"
+        "title": "Install cert-manager",
+        "description": "Install cert-manager to handle TLS certificate lifecycle management for kcp components:
```bash
helm repo add jetstack https://charts.jetstack.io
helm repo update
helm upgrade --install --namespace cert-manager --create-namespace --version v1.18.2 --set crds.enabled=true --atomic cert-manager jetstack/cert-manager
```",
+        "commands": [
+          "helm repo add jetstack https://charts.jetstack.io",
+          "helm repo update",
+          "helm upgrade --install --namespace cert-manager --create-namespace --version v1.18.2 --set crds.enabled=true --atomic cert-manager jetstack/cert-manager"
+        ],
+        "validation": {
+          "command": "kubectl get pods --namespace cert-manager",
+          "expected": "Running"
+        },
+        "rollback": [
+          "helm uninstall cert-manager --namespace cert-manager",
+          "kubectl delete namespace cert-manager"
+        ]
       },
       {
-        "title": "Install kcp using Helm",
-        "description": "Install kcp in a new namespace called 'kcp-system':
```bash
helm install kcp kcp/kcp --namespace kcp-system --create-namespace --version v0.30.0
```"
+        "title": "Install etcd-druid operator",
+        "description": "Install the etcd-druid operator from the Gardener OCI registry to manage etcd clusters for kcp:
```bash
helm install etcd-druid oci://europe-docker.pkg.dev/gardener-project/releases/charts/gardener/etcd-druid --namespace etcd-druid --create-namespace --version v0.33.0
```",
+        "commands": [
+          "helm install etcd-druid oci://europe-docker.pkg.dev/gardener-project/releases/charts/gardener/etcd-druid --namespace etcd-druid --create-namespace --version v0.33.0"
+        ],
+        "validation": {
+          "command": "kubectl get pods --namespace etcd-druid",
+          "expected": "Running"
+        },
+        "rollback": [
+          "helm uninstall etcd-druid --namespace etcd-druid",
+          "kubectl delete namespace etcd-druid"
+        ]
       },
       {
-        "title": "Verify the installation",
-        "description": "Check that the kcp pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kcp-system
```"
+        "title": "Install kcp-operator",
+        "description": "Add the kcp Helm repository and install the kcp-operator:
```bash
helm repo add kcp https://kcp-dev.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm upgrade --install --create-namespace --namespace kcp-operator kcp-operator kcp/kcp-operator
```",
+        "commands": [
+          "helm repo add kcp https://kcp-dev.github.io/helm-charts",
+          "helm repo update",
+          "helm upgrade --install --create-namespace --namespace kcp-operator kcp-operator kcp/kcp-operator"
+        ],
+        "validation": {
+          "command": "kubectl get pods --namespace kcp-operator",
+          "expected": "Running"
+        },
+        "rollback": [
+          "helm uninstall kcp-operator --namespace kcp-operator",
+          "kubectl delete namespace kcp-operator"
+        ]
       },
       {
-        "title": "Set up RBAC for kcp",
-        "description": "Create a ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding for kcp:
```bash
kubectl create clusterrole kcp-admin --verb=* --resource=* 

kubectl create clusterrolebinding kcp-admin-binding --clusterrole=kcp-admin --serviceaccount=kcp-system:kcp
```"
+        "title": "Create certificate issuer",
+        "description": "Create a self-signed certificate issuer for kcp TLS certificates:
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
kind: Issuer
metadata:
  name: selfsigned
  namespace: kcp-system
spec:
  selfSigned: {}
EOF
```",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl create namespace kcp-system",
+          "kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
kind: Issuer
metadata:
  name: selfsigned
  namespace: kcp-system
spec:
  selfSigned: {}
EOF"
+        ],
+        "validation": {
+          "command": "kubectl get issuer selfsigned --namespace kcp-system",
+          "expected": "True"
+        },
+        "rollback": [
+          "kubectl delete issuer selfsigned --namespace kcp-system"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Deploy RootShard",
+        "description": "Deploy the kcp RootShard custom resource with embedded cache. Replace 'kcp.example.com' with your actual hostname:
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: operator.kcp.io/v1alpha1
kind: RootShard
metadata:
  name: root
  namespace: kcp-system
spec:
  external:
    hostname: kcp.example.com
    port: 6443
  certificates:
    issuerRef:
      group: cert-manager.io
      kind: Issuer
      name: selfsigned
  cache:
    embedded:
      enabled: true
  etcd:
    endpoints:
      - http://etcd.kcp-system.svc.cluster.local:2379
EOF
```",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operator.kcp.io/v1alpha1
kind: RootShard
metadata:
  name: root
  namespace: kcp-system
spec:
  external:
    hostname: kcp.example.com
    port: 6443
  certificates:
    issuerRef:
      group: cert-manager.io
      kind: Issuer
      name: selfsigned
  cache:
    embedded:
      enabled: true
  etcd:
    endpoints:
      - http://etcd.kcp-system.svc.cluster.local:2379
EOF"
+        ],
+        "validation": {
+          "command": "kubectl get rootshard root --namespace kcp-system -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Ready")].status}'",
+          "expected": "True"
+        },
+        "rollback": [
+          "kubectl delete rootshard root --namespace kcp-system"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Deploy FrontProxy",
+        "description": "Deploy the kcp FrontProxy custom resource to expose the kcp API:
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: operator.kcp.io/v1alpha1
kind: FrontProxy
metadata:
  name: frontproxy
  namespace: kcp-system
spec:
  rootShard:
    ref:
      name: root
  serviceTemplate:
    spec:
      type: LoadBalancer
EOF
```",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operator.kcp.io/v1alpha1
kind: FrontProxy
metadata:
  name: frontproxy
  namespace: kcp-system
spec:
  rootShard:
    ref:
      name: root
  serviceTemplate:
    spec:
      type: LoadBalancer
EOF"
+        ],
+        "validation": {
+          "command": "kubectl get frontproxy frontproxy --namespace kcp-system -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Ready")].status}'",
+          "expected": "True"
+        },
+        "rollback": [
+          "kubectl delete frontproxy frontproxy --namespace kcp-system"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Generate admin kubeconfig",
+        "description": "Create a Kubeconfig custom resource to generate admin credentials, then extract the kubeconfig from the resulting secret:
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: operator.kcp.io/v1alpha1
kind: Kubeconfig
metadata:
  name: kcp-admin
  namespace: kcp-system
spec:
  username: kcp-admin
  groups:
    - system:kcp:admin
  validity: 8766h
  secretRef:
    name: admin-kubeconfig
  target:
    frontProxyRef:
      name: frontproxy
EOF

kubectl get secret admin-kubeconfig --namespace kcp-system -o jsonpath='{.data.kubeconfig}' | base64 -d > kcp-admin.kubeconfig
export KUBECONFIG=$(pwd)/kcp-admin.kubeconfig
```",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operator.kcp.io/v1alpha1
kind: Kubeconfig
metadata:
  name: kcp-admin
  namespace: kcp-system
spec:
  username: kcp-admin
  groups:
    - system:kcp:admin
  validity: 8766h
  secretRef:
    name: admin-kubeconfig
  target:
    frontProxyRef:
      name: frontproxy
EOF",
+          "kubectl get secret admin-kubeconfig --namespace kcp-system -o jsonpath='{.data.kubeconfig}' | base64 -d > kcp-admin.kubeconfig",
+          "export KUBECONFIG=$(pwd)/kcp-admin.kubeconfig"
+        ],
+        "validation": {
+          "command": "kubectl get secret admin-kubeconfig --namespace kcp-system",
+          "expected": "admin-kubeconfig"
+        },
+        "rollback": [
+          "kubectl delete kubeconfig kcp-admin --namespace kcp-system",
+          "kubectl delete secret admin-kubeconfig --namespace kcp-system",
+          "rm -f kcp-admin.kubeconfig"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify kcp is accessible",
+        "description": "Verify the kcp installation by checking the health endpoint and listing shards:
```bash
curl -k https://kcp.example.com:6443/healthz
KUBECONFIG=kcp-admin.kubeconfig kubectl get shards
KUBECONFIG=kcp-admin.kubeconfig kubectl ws
```",
+        "commands": [
+          "curl -k https://kcp.example.com:6443/healthz",
+          "KUBECONFIG=kcp-admin.kubeconfig kubectl get shards",
+          "KUBECONFIG=kcp-admin.kubeconfig kubectl ws"
+        ],
+        "validation": {
+          "command": "KUBECONFIG=kcp-admin.kubeconfig kubectl get shards",
+          "expected": "root"
+        },
+        "rollback": []
       }
     ],
     "resolution": {
-      "summary": "A successful installation of kcp will show the kcp pods running in the 'kcp-system' namespace. You will also have configured resource limits and RBAC for the kcp deployment, ensuring it operates efficiently and securely.",
+      "summary": "A successful operator-based installation of kcp will show the kcp-operator, RootShard, and FrontProxy pods running. The admin kubeconfig extracted from the Kubeconfig CR secret enables access to the kcp API. You can verify by running 'kubectl get shards' which should show the root shard, and 'kubectl ws' to list workspaces.",
       "codeSnippets": [
-        "helm repo add kcp https://kcp-dev.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update",
-        "helm install kcp kcp/kcp --namespace kcp-system --create-namespace --version v0.30.0",
-        "kubectl get pods --namespace kcp-system",
-        "kubectl edit deployment kcp --namespace kcp-system
# Add the following under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   requests:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"
#   limits:
#     cpu: "1"
#     memory: "1Gi"",
-        "kubectl create clusterrole kcp-admin --verb=* --resource=* 

kubectl create clusterrolebinding kcp-admin-binding --clusterrole=kcp-admin --serviceaccount=kcp-system:kcp"
+        "helm repo add jetstack https://charts.jetstack.io
helm repo update
helm upgrade --install --namespace cert-manager --create-namespace --version v1.18.2 --set crds.enabled=true --atomic cert-manager jetstack/cert-manager",
+        "helm install etcd-druid oci://europe-docker.pkg.dev/gardener-project/releases/charts/gardener/etcd-druid --namespace etcd-druid --create-namespace --version v0.33.0",
+        "helm repo add kcp https://kcp-dev.github.io/helm-charts
helm upgrade --install --create-namespace --namespace kcp-operator kcp-operator kcp/kcp-operator",
+        "kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
kind: Issuer
metadata:
  name: selfsigned
  namespace: kcp-system
spec:
  selfSigned: {}
EOF",
+        "kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operator.kcp.io/v1alpha1
kind: RootShard
metadata:
  name: root
  namespace: kcp-system
spec:
  external:
    hostname: kcp.example.com
    port: 6443
  certificates:
    issuerRef:
      group: cert-manager.io
      kind: Issuer
      name: selfsigned
  cache:
    embedded:
      enabled: true
  etcd:
    endpoints:
      - http://etcd.kcp-system.svc.cluster.local:2379
EOF",
+        "kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operator.kcp.io/v1alpha1
kind: FrontProxy
metadata:
  name: frontproxy
  namespace: kcp-system
spec:
  rootShard:
    ref:
      name: root
  serviceTemplate:
    spec:
      type: LoadBalancer
EOF",
+        "kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operator.kcp.io/v1alpha1
kind: Kubeconfig
metadata:
  name: kcp-admin
  namespace: kcp-system
spec:
  username: kcp-admin
  groups:
    - system:kcp:admin
  validity: 8766h
  secretRef:
    name: admin-kubeconfig
  target:
    frontProxyRef:
      name: frontproxy
EOF",
+        "kubectl get secret admin-kubeconfig --namespace kcp-system -o jsonpath='{.data.kubeconfig}' | base64 -d > kcp-admin.kubeconfig
KUBECONFIG=kcp-admin.kubeconfig kubectl get shards"
       ]
     },
     "uninstall": [
       {
-        "title": "Remove the Helm release",
-        "description": "Uninstall the kcp release from the kcp-system namespace using Helm to remove the deployment and associated resources.
```bash
helm uninstall kcp --namespace kcp-system
```"
+        "title": "Remove admin kubeconfig and Kubeconfig CR",
+        "description": "Delete the Kubeconfig custom resource and the generated admin kubeconfig file.
```bash
kubectl delete kubeconfig kcp-admin --namespace kcp-system
kubectl delete secret admin-kubeconfig --namespace kcp-system
rm -f kcp-admin.kubeconfig
```"
       },
       {
-        "title": "Clean up CRDs and persistent resources",
-        "description": "Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) that were created during the installation.
```bash
kubectl delete crd <crd-name>
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace kcp-system
```"
+        "title": "Remove FrontProxy and RootShard",
+        "description": "Delete the FrontProxy and RootShard custom resources. The kcp-operator will clean up the associated pods and services.
```bash
kubectl delete frontproxy frontproxy --namespace kcp-system
kubectl delete rootshard root --namespace kcp-system
```"
       },
       {
-        "title": "Remove namespace and verify",
-        "description": "Delete the kcp-system namespace and verify that it has been removed successfully.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kcp-system
kubectl get namespaces
```"
+        "title": "Remove certificate issuer and namespace",
+        "description": "Delete the self-signed issuer and the kcp-system namespace.
```bash
kubectl delete issuer selfsigned --namespace kcp-system
kubectl delete namespace kcp-system
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Uninstall kcp-operator",
+        "description": "Remove the kcp-operator Helm release and its namespace.
```bash
helm uninstall kcp-operator --namespace kcp-operator
kubectl delete namespace kcp-operator
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Uninstall etcd-druid",
+        "description": "Remove the etcd-druid Helm release and its namespace.
```bash
helm uninstall etcd-druid --namespace etcd-druid
kubectl delete namespace etcd-druid
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Uninstall cert-manager (optional)",
+        "description": "Remove cert-manager if it is no longer needed by other workloads.
```bash
helm uninstall cert-manager --namespace cert-manager
kubectl delete namespace cert-manager
```"
       }
     ],
     "upgrade": [
       {
         "title": "Backup current state",
-        "description": "Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and resources in the kcp-system namespace.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace kcp-system -o yaml > kcp-backup.yaml
```"
+        "description": "Before upgrading, back up the current kcp custom resources and configuration.
```bash
kubectl get rootshard,frontproxy,kubeconfig --namespace kcp-system -o yaml > kcp-backup.yaml
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade kcp-operator",
+        "description": "Update the Helm repository and upgrade the kcp-operator to the latest version. The operator will reconcile the RootShard and FrontProxy resources automatically.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade --namespace kcp-operator kcp-operator kcp/kcp-operator
```"
       },
       {
-        "title": "Update repo and run upgrade command",
-        "description": "Update the Helm repository and upgrade the kcp release to the latest version.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kcp kcp/kcp --namespace kcp-system --version v0.31.0
```"
+        "title": "Upgrade etcd-druid (if needed)",
+        "description": "Upgrade etcd-druid to a newer version if required.
```bash
helm upgrade etcd-druid oci://europe-docker.pkg.dev/gardener-project/releases/charts/gardener/etcd-druid --namespace etcd-druid --version <new-version>
```"
       },
       {
         "title": "Verify the upgrade",
-        "description": "Check that the kcp pods are running the new version after the upgrade.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kcp-system
```"
+        "description": "Check that all kcp pods are running the new version and the shards are healthy.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kcp-operator
kubectl get pods --namespace kcp-system
KUBECONFIG=kcp-admin.kubeconfig kubectl get shards
```"
       }
     ],
     "troubleshooting": [
       {
-        "title": "Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff",
-        "description": "If kcp pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors that might indicate the cause.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace kcp-system
```"
+        "title": "RootShard not becoming ready",
+        "description": "If the RootShard is stuck in a non-ready state, check the kcp-operator logs and the RootShard status conditions.
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp-operator --namespace kcp-operator
kubectl describe rootshard root --namespace kcp-system
```"
       },
       {
-        "title": "Helm upgrade fails with version conflict",
-        "description": "If you encounter a version conflict during upgrade, ensure that the specified version is available in the Helm repository.
```bash
helm search repo kcp/kcp --versions
```"
+        "title": "FrontProxy LoadBalancer pending",
+        "description": "If the FrontProxy service is stuck with a pending external IP, ensure your cluster supports LoadBalancer services or switch to NodePort.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace kcp-system
kubectl describe frontproxy frontproxy --namespace kcp-system
```"
       },
       {
-        "title": "RBAC permissions issues",
-        "description": "If you face permission issues, verify that the ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding are correctly set up for the kcp service account.
```bash
kubectl get clusterrolebindings | grep kcp-admin-binding
```"
+        "title": "cert-manager issuer not ready",
+        "description": "If the certificate issuer is not becoming ready, check cert-manager logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl get issuer --namespace kcp-system
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager --namespace cert-manager
```"
       },
       {
-        "title": "Namespace not found error",
-        "description": "If you receive a namespace not found error, ensure that the kcp-system namespace exists and is correctly referenced in your commands.
```bash
kubectl get namespaces
```"
+        "title": "etcd connection failures",
+        "description": "If the RootShard reports etcd connection issues, verify the etcd pods are running and the endpoint is correct.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kcp-system -l app=etcd-statefulset
kubectl logs -l app=etcd-statefulset --namespace kcp-system
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Admin kubeconfig secret not created",
+        "description": "If the admin-kubeconfig secret is not appearing, check the Kubeconfig CR status and ensure the FrontProxy is ready.
```bash
kubectl describe kubeconfig kcp-admin --namespace kcp-system
kubectl get frontproxy frontproxy --namespace kcp-system
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "CRDs not installed",
+        "description": "If the operator reports missing CRDs, ensure the kcp-operator Helm chart was installed correctly. You can also apply CRDs manually.
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep kcp
kubectl get crd | grep druid
```"
       }
     ]
   },
@@ -90,7 +225,8 @@
       "configuration",
       "cncf",
       "app-definition",
-      "sandbox"
+      "sandbox",
+      "operator"
     ],
     "cncfProjects": [
       "kcp"
@@ -99,40 +235,47 @@
       "Deployment",
       "Service",
       "Namespace",
-      "Serviceaccount",
-      "Clusterrole",
-      "Clusterrolebinding"
+      "RootShard",
+      "FrontProxy",
+      "Kubeconfig",
+      "Issuer",
+      "Certificate"
     ],
     "difficulty": "intermediate",
     "issueTypes": [
       "installation",
       "configuration"
     ],
     "installMethods": [
-      "helm"
+      "helm",
+      "operator"
     ],
     "maturity": "sandbox",
     "projectVersion": "v0.30.0",
     "containerImages": [
-      "docker.io/golang:1.24.13"
+      "ghcr.io/kcp-dev/kcp",
+      "ghcr.io/kcp-dev/kcp-operator"
     ],
     "sourceUrls": {
-      "docs": "https://kcp.io",
+      "docs": "https://docs.kcp.io/kcp/v0.30/setup/production/",
       "repo": "https://github.com/kcp-dev/kcp",
-      "helm": "https://kcp-dev.github.io/helm-charts"
+      "helm": "https://kcp-dev.github.io/helm-charts",
+      "operator-docs": "https://docs.kcp.io/kcp-operator/v0.4/",
+      "production-assets": "https://github.com/kcp-dev/kcp/tree/main/contrib/production"
     },
     "qualityScore": 100
   },
   "prerequisites": {
     "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
     "tools": [
       "helm",
-      "kubectl"
+      "kubectl",
+      "curl"
     ],
-    "description": "Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine."
+    "description": "Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher with Helm 3 and kubectl installed. The cluster must support LoadBalancer services (or use NodePort as an alternative). cert-manager, etcd-druid, and the kcp-operator will be installed as part of the mission steps."
   },
   "security": {
-    "scannedAt": "2026-03-01T16:15:11.894Z",
+    "scannedAt": "2026-04-07T00:00:00.000Z",
     "scannerVersion": "cncf-install-gen-1.0.0",
     "sanitized": true,
     "findings": []
